episode_text:
day=13; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Social with person on Right; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or social with a person on the right THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to reheat cold fruit meals across multiple non-quiet contexts, including repeated right-side social fruit meals, consistent with fruit being preferred unheated. IF food_condition is warm savory food such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right or social with a person in front or on the right THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected moderate reheating for non-fruit meals across several contexts. IF food_condition is savory food with sauce or dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop in front THEN 1 min or 3 min, with evidence indicating variability in that specific laptop-front sauced-food context rather than a single fixed reheating level, BECAUSE the user selected both lighter and stronger reheating there. IF food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the left THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected lighter reheating in that left-TV context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the view toward a front-facing social partner. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or fruit THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the front view while working across different foods. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the view toward a right-side social partner across multiple meals and foods, now with additional repeated fruit evidence. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the view toward the TV on the right. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the left-side view in that entertainment context. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user also wants to preserve visibility toward a right-side work display.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken with hash brown THEN medium BECAUSE the user explicitly accepted a moderate pace in that social meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN slow or medium, with repeated evidence now favoring slow for fruit and slow for sauced food while medium has also been observed in a right-side social fruit meal, BECAUSE the user appears somewhat cautious in right-side social contexts and especially willing to slow down there. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted a moderate pace there. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch and potato wedges THEN medium or fast, with evidence of a faster pace being acceptable in that task-focused context, BECAUSE the user previously selected both medium and fast there. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN medium BECAUSE the evidence supports a moderate pace in a front-laptop fruit context. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN medium BECAUSE the evidence supports a moderate pace in a right-side work context. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN fast BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the fastest pace in that entertainment context, indicating willingness to prioritize efficiency there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally accepts more speed in solitary task or TV contexts and tends to be more cautious in social right-side contexts.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken strips, buffalo chicken bites, or fruit pieces AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, working on a laptop in front, or working on a laptop on the right THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option, which is compatible with limited mouth opening, across both savory finger foods and fruit, with additional repeated support from right-side social fruit meals. IF food_condition is savory mixed pieces such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the left THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-acquisition-success option for that meal, indicating a stable food-specific exception despite the general narrow-bite preference.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, fruit, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, working on a laptop in front, or working on a laptop on the right THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred explicit confirmation of bite acquisition rather than skipping verification, including repeated right-side social fruit meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF user has limited trunk control and cannot lean forward AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type so far THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer is poorly matched to the user's physical abilities.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward, making inside-mouth transfer the physically compatible mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, working on a laptop in front, watching TV on the left, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, fruit, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN LED BECAUSE the accumulated evidence supports LED-only signaling across all observed contexts, including repeated right-side social fruit meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and avoids unreliable open-mouth signaling, especially in social interaction, with repeated support in right-side social fruit meals. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, working on a laptop in front, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or fruit THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable low-ambiguity readiness method in attention-demanding contexts. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN button or autocontinue, with repeated evidence showing variability within that same left-TV context, BECAUSE the user has selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally favors explicit button control, while some non-social TV contexts can permit more automation.

IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and avoids mouth-based readiness detection that may be unreliable for this user, with repeated support in right-side social fruit meals. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, working on a laptop in front, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or fruit THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable low-ambiguity readiness method. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN button or autocontinue, with repeated evidence showing variability in that same left-TV context, BECAUSE the user has selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally favors explicit button control, while some non-social TV contexts can permit more automation.

IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and more reliable than mouth-based signaling, with repeated support in right-side social fruit meals. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, working on a laptop in front, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or fruit THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable readiness method. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN button or autocontinue, with repeated evidence showing variability in that same left-TV context, BECAUSE the user has selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally favors explicit button control, while some non-social TV contexts can permit more automation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, working on a laptop in front, watching TV on the left, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, fruit, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN LED BECAUSE the accumulated evidence supports LED-only completion signaling across all observed contexts, including repeated right-side social fruit meals. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food type observed so far THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently accepted the reliable low-effort completion method for bites across observed contexts, including repeated right-side social fruit meals.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND user cannot reliably perform head gestures AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right, watching TV on the right, working on a laptop in front, watching TV on the left, or working on a laptop on the right AND food_condition is any observed food type in those contexts THEN button BECAUSE perception for drinking depends on head-nod gestures, which are not physically reliable for this user, and the repeated evidence supports explicit manual completion across all observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet beyond the stable avoidance of gesture-based perception.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND user cannot reliably perform head gestures AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right, watching TV on the right, working on a laptop in front, watching TV on the left, or working on a laptop on the right AND food_condition is any observed food type in those contexts THEN button BECAUSE perception for wiping depends on head-nod gestures, which are not physically reliable for this user, and the repeated evidence supports explicit manual completion across all observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet beyond the stable avoidance of gesture-based perception.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred retracting in front-facing social meals to reduce prolonged visual obstruction. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred retracting in right-side social settings across multiple meals, including repeated fruit meals, indicating a broader tendency to reduce obstruction during social meals. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch and potato wedges or fruit THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred retracting while engaged with a front-facing task, consistent with wanting to keep the workspace visible. IF dining context is watching TV with the TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to retract in that entertainment context despite caring about right-side occlusion, indicating a preference to avoid extra meal time there. IF dining context is watching TV with the TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to retract in that entertainment context, reinforcing a general TV-viewing preference to avoid extra delay. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN no BECAUSE the evidence shows that in a right-side work context the user can prefer preserving efficiency over retracting, even while still wanting to minimize right-side occlusion.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user accepted some dipping while limiting messiness in social contexts. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred limited dipping, consistent with reducing messiness during an attention-demanding front-focused activity. IF food has no dip or sauce available AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context so far, including watching TV on the left, watching TV on the right, social settings, and working on a laptop in front or on the right THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for non-dippable meals, including fruit, sandwiches without dip use, chicken with hash brown, and General Tso's chicken and broccoli.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, working on a laptop in front, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, fruit, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the longest wait, reinforcing a stable preference to avoid automatic progression without ample time to intervene in those contexts, including repeated right-side social fruit meals. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a very short wait in that left-TV context, indicating a context-specific preference for rapid automation there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally prefers long waits when maintaining explicit control, but accepts short waits in a non-social TV context where faster progression is acceptable.
